content.problemStatement:
During a boring night at the restaurant, Chef decided to play with spaghetti. He has R−L+1R-L+1R−L+1 spaghetti: exactly one spaghetti with each length between LLL and RRR inclusive.

Chef decided to take some of his spaghetti and stick them together to form one very long spaghetti. The length of this spaghetti is the sum of the lengths of the spaghetti he took. In particular, he may take all spaghetti and he may take only one spaghetti as well. How many different spaghetti can he make?

Two spaghetti are considered different if their lengths are different.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of different spaghetti Chef can make.
